justifications.ip1: All three ACs have specific failing test scenarios: AC1 covered by T1–T3 (PII fields set to '[REDACTED]', non-PII preserved, 89-day untouched), AC2 by T5–T7 (365-day deleted, 364-day redacted not deleted), AC3 by T8–T14 (audit entries with correct fields per acted-upon record).
justifications.ip2: No out-of-scope items are implemented — the plan explicitly excludes cron/scheduling ('Scheduling/cron wiring is out of scope'), consent workflow, attachment redaction, Azure DPA, and configurable thresholds; C4 is directly reflected with REDACT_AFTER_DAYS and PURGE_AFTER_DAYS as local compiled-in constants.
justifications.ip3: Every task names specific files (`tests/crm/transcript-retention.test.js`, `src/crm/transcript-retention.js`, `src/crm/retention-audit-store.js`), concrete function names (`enforceRetention`, `transcriptStore.findById`, `retentionAuditStore.append`), and exact assertion values (`'[REDACTED]'`, `toBeNull()`).
justifications.ip4: Task 1 writes all failing tests first with an explicit RED confirmation ('All tests fail with Cannot find module'), and Task 3 is gated with 'Only write this file after Task 1 tests are committed and confirmed RED', preserving strict test-first order throughout.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1's full 89/90/91/364/365/366-day boundary matrix is covered by both individual tests (T4–T7) and a parameterised boundary suite; C4 constants are local, unexported, and annotated 'Do NOT extract to config or accept as parameters', with a note explaining why they are not exported.

notes: Exemplary plan: every AC has precise failing-test assertions, C4 compiled-in constants are correctly local and unexported, NFR-1 boundary matrix is exhaustive, and TDD RED-before-GREEN gating is explicit and enforced.
